checklist: Implement Singly and Doubly Linked Lists
Evaluate time complexities of array insertions
Handle linked list pointer updates safely

states.question: What is the time complexity of deleting the first element in a standard Array of size N?
states.explanation: Deleting the first array element requires shifting all remaining N-1 elements left, taking O(N) linear time.

states.analogy_description: An array is like a row of movie theater seats. Inserting someone in the middle requires shifting everyone down. A linked list is like a train: nodes link to the next. Inserting a train car only requires uncoupling and re-pointing.

whiteboard_data.title: Singly Linked List Nodes
whiteboard_data.description: Nodes linking sequentially via next pointers.

states.exercise_description: We have defined a basic Node class. Write a function `get_val(node)` that returns the value of that node.
Write a function `length_of_list(head)` that takes the head Node of a singly linked list and returns its length. Traverse until `next` is `None`.

states.prompt: Explain Elementary Data Structures in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.front: What is the key advantage of a Doubly Linked List over a Singly Linked List?
What are Stack and Queue insertion terms?
